title: North American GeoGebra Journal Article Metadata
description: Metadata schema for published and submitted NAGJ articles. Validates the YAML front matter of files in the _articles collection. NOTE: this describes JSON types. YAML parsers coerce unquoted ISO dates (date: 2012-01-01) into date objects, which are not JSON strings -- the loader must normalize dates to ISO strings before validating.

slug.description: Stable identifier: lastname + year + keyword, ASCII-only and lowercased. Non-ASCII surnames are transliterated (Gunčaga -> guncaga).

issue.description: NAGJ has only ever published one issue per volume; this is effectively always 1.

year.description: The journal's first issue was Volume 1, Number 1 (2012).

date.description: ISO date. Quote it in YAML ('2012-01-01') or normalize before validating.

pages.description: Page range within the issue, e.g. '45--48'. Null for the 53 legacy articles whose range was never recorded; required for status: published.

status.description: 'archived' = legacy article migrated from the OJS era (metadata record + original PDF). 'published' = GitHub-native article with Markdown source and an open review record.

license.description: SPDX identifiers. CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 covers most of the legacy archive; CC-BY-SA-4.0 covers a few 2016-era papers. Null is permitted for legacy records whose terms are unrecorded.

pdf.description: Site-absolute path. Volume is zero-padded to two digits; the issue is not (paths are v01/n1/, matching actual journal practice).

geogebra.description: Permanent URLs to GeoGebra materials. Null or omitted when the article has none.

source_repository.description: The author's manuscript repository. Null for legacy articles, which predate the GitHub workflow.

review_issue.description: Permanent URL of the open review issue. Null for legacy articles.

citation_key.description: BibTeX key. Must equal the slug; enforced by validate_metadata.py.

name.description: Full name as printed, with diacritics preserved (e.g. 'Ján Gunčaga').

orcid.description: Null when unknown. Never invent or placeholder-fill this field.

then.comment: pages is NOT required here: 19 of the 89 legacy articles never recorded a page range, and inventing one would be worse than omitting it. New articles must supply it -- see the published-only rule below.
